assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends using 'Release Channel + No Minor or Node Upgrades Exclusion' as the primary approach and provides gcloud commands for this configuration

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: The response specifically recommends the 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' exclusion scope and includes the --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support flag with explanation that it auto-tracks EoS

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: The response includes a dedicated section on maintenance window configuration with a specific example showing Saturday 2-6 AM maintenance window using gcloud commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: The response does not mention cluster disruption budget or disruption intervals with their specific default and maximum values

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: The response recommends Stable channel for production and includes Extended channel in the comparison table, noting it's for compliance-heavy environments with 24mo support and extra cost during extended period

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: The response emphasizes that control plane security patches are auto-applied and lists this as a security compliance benefit, though it doesn't explicitly state the monthly minimum recommendation

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: While the response mentions multi-environment strategy with dev/staging/prod progression, it does not discuss rollout sequencing or cluster disruption budget for controlling control plane patch frequency

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response provides multiple gcloud commands including specific commands for adding maintenance exclusions with the no_minor_or_node_upgrades scope and configuring maintenance windows
